title: 'Has severely hit projects': Govt tightens Isro exit rules as 120 scientists quit in a year
content: Isro's Department of Space has introduced new rules for scientist resignations. These new regulations aim to prevent departures from key projects like Gaganyaan. The department now requires central directors to refer all such requests to DoS. This move reverses a 2020 decision that empowered centre directors to accept resignations. Isro acknowledges departures but states it is prepared to manage the situation.

title: Bengaluru law student dies after man stabs her for rejecting his brother's marriage proposal
content: A law student died after being stabbed by the brother of a man whose marriage proposal she rejected. The accused attacked the victim at her residence after she ended their relationship. He allegedly used a knife during the altercation, inflicting serious chest and back wounds. Local residents apprehended the attacker immediately after the violent incident occurred. Police have arrested both brothers and are continuing their thorough investigation.

title: Gill's chase at Edgbaston showed why India already have Kohli's ODI heir
content: Shubman Gill's unbeaten 80 against England in the first ODI at Edgbaston was a lesson in calculated ODI batting rather than outright aggression. On a seamer-friendly surface, he absorbed pressure, rebuilt after India's early setbacks and accelerated at the right moments to steer the chase. Even cramps couldn't derail his composure, reinforcing why Gill, who is still uncertain for the second ODI in Cardiff, is rapidly becoming India's premier chase specialist, much like Virat Kohli.

title: 'They better behave': Trump says Iran wants settlement amid fresh strikes
content: President Trump warned Iran to behave if it desires negotiations with Washington. US forces conducted new military strikes targeting Iranian command centers and air defense systems. These strikes aimed to degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian authorities reported a missile attack near a cancer hospital in Ahvaz. Damage was also reported at an industrial facility in Qeshm following an enemy attack.

title: Dimon shares list of leadership traits that he wants to see in JPMorgan's next CEO
content: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon detailed extensive leadership requirements for his successor.  He emphasized a blend of analytical skills, work ethic, and human qualities.  The next leader must navigate global politics and understand complex back-office functions.  Dimon expressed confidence in the bank's deep talent pool for the transition.  He currently has no immediate plans for retirement from his leadership role.

title: Are banks closed today for Rath Yatra and Harela? Check state-wise list
content: Bank branches will be closed in Odisha, Uttarakhand, and Manipur on July 16. These closures are for Ratha Yatra and the Harela festival celebrations. Customers can still use online and mobile banking services for transactions. Other regional holidays are scheduled later this month across various states. Regular banking operations will resume on the next working day.

title: SUV at 140kmph crashes on Mumbai-Goa highway; 3 of family and driver dead
content: A newly married couple's family faced tragedy returning from Goa. Their SUV crashed into a parked dumper truck on the Mumbai-Goa highway. Four family members and the driver died in the devastating accident. The groom sustained severe injuries and is hospitalized for treatment. Police are investigating the dangerously parked truck and the speeding SUV.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the organization's history. Active Minds works with young people to improve mental health awareness and reduce stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support youth leaders. Scott continues her extensive charitable giving after selling Amazon shares.

title: Man charged with murder after Indian woman stabbed to death at home in London
content: A 24-year-old Indian woman was fatally stabbed at her London home. A man has been charged with murder and attempted murder in connection with the incident. The victim's family alleges the attack was racially motivated and seeks government assistance. They had sold land to fund her education and future aspirations in the UK. British Sikh groups express concern over rising race-based attacks targeting vulnerable individuals.

title: Farm left without crops for 15 years turns into wildflower-rich meadow
content: An abandoned Norfolk farm field transformed into a vibrant wildflower meadow over a decade, as researchers monitored its evolution post-farming. Within ten to fifteen years, the area demonstrated an impressive resurgence of various plant species, emphasizing that simple, natural restoration techniques may outperform costly commercial methods. This evidence underscores the value of patience in reviving ecosystems and restoring lost habitats effectively.

title: Scientist finds demon cavefish in pool beneath US military base
content: In a remarkable finding, a scientist has uncovered a new species of eyeless fish in Bobcat Cave, aptly named the demon cavefish after a character from a popular TV series. This fascinating creature has evolved to thrive in complete darkness, displaying no eyes or pigmentation. Intriguingly, it cohabits with another sightless predator, showcasing the resilience of unique wildlife beneath humanity's constructed environments.

title: After Grok 'caught' uploading customer code, Altman 'shames' Musk’s SpaceX
content: Elon Musk pledged total transparency for X's codebase after a data leak. xAI's Grok Build tool uploaded extensive private customer code repositories. This excessive data collection raised concerns among security experts and rivals. The company has since deleted all collected user data as a precaution. Musk will now make X's entire codebase open source for review.

title: 'Not in good shape but ...': Sonam Wangchuk refuses to end fast despite health concerns
content: Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike for nineteen days, stating his health is not critical. He urged supporters to join a Parliament march on July 20 instead of asking him to break his fast. Wangchuk believes his protest sends a message about government accountability and the need for action. His health has been monitored, and medical tests show stable results for his condition.

title: Explained: What is Argentina's 'Las Malvinas' Falkland Islands controversy?
content: Argentina's World Cup semifinal victory over England has ignited a fresh controversy.  Midfielder Giovani Lo Celso displayed a banner claiming the Falkland Islands are Argentine.  FIFA rules prohibit political messages during football matches, potentially leading to disciplinary action.  The Falkland Islands have been a subject of dispute between Argentina and the United Kingdom for decades.  Argentina's Foreign Ministry recently reaffirmed its claim over the islands in an official communiqué.

title: Messi's reaction after Argentina players find England goalkeeper's secret penalty notes
content: Lionel Messi and Argentina teammates sparked a viral moment after their World Cup semi-final victory over England, as they inspected Jordan Pickford's penalty notes taped to his water bottle. The England goalkeeper's preparations for a shootout went unused after Lautaro Martínez's stoppage-time winner sealed a 2-1 comeback. Messi's amused reaction quickly became one of the tournament's most talked-about moments.

title: Jon Gruden on Antonio Brown: 'He finished everything' and NFL's future
content: Jon Gruden discussed his biggest coaching regret, the Antonio Brown situation with the Raiders. He expressed concern over modern NFL offenses, which he feels lack communication. ​During a recent appearance on Cam Heyward's Not Just Football podcast, the Super Bowl winning coach reflected on one of the biggest disappointments of his career and shared why he believes today's NFL is moving in the wrong direction.

title: Roman Reigns vs Seth Rollins net worth 2026: Salary, brand deals compared
content: Roman Reigns and Seth Rollins will clash at SummerSlam 2026 in a highly anticipated match. Reigns boasts an estimated net worth of twenty million dollars and earns fifteen million annually. Rollins holds an estimated net worth between nine and twelve million dollars, earning seven million. Both wrestlers have lucrative WWE contracts and significant merchandise sales contributing to their wealth.

title: Erling Haaland's 'five girlfriends' comment: The viral twist explained
content: Erling Haaland's old "five girlfriends" comment has resurfaced, surprising fans years after it first made headlines. While many initially thought the Norway striker was talking about his dating life, he was actually referring to the five footballs he kept after scoring hat-tricks. The lighthearted remark highlighted his obsession with scoring goals long before he became a global superstar, Manchester City's leading striker and one of football's highest-paid players.

title: UP horror: Man kills wife, buries her under mud floor, sleeps over spot for days
content: Police exhumed a woman's body from her husband's home after she was allegedly murdered. Her husband buried her body inside the house and plastered the floor. He then filed a missing person report to divert suspicion from himself. The woman's mother had alleged her son-in-law and his parents killed her daughter. An FIR was registered, and teams are working to arrest the accused.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: Anant Raj, Sobha Ltd, and PNB Housing Finance have been identified as the top stocks to buy by Aakash K Hindocha, Vice President - Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group/Nuvama Wealth Management for July 16, 2026. Views on Nifty and Bank Nifty have also been shared:

title: Paresh Rawal regrets calling Anupama Chopra ‘Miss Irrelevant’ over her ‘Dhurandhar’ review
content: Paresh Rawal, the acclaimed veteran actor, has expressed regret over his social media jab aimed at Anupama Chopra's critique of his film Dhurandhar. While he still holds a differing opinion on her review, he acknowledges that his reaction was inappropriate. Emphasizing the need for constructive criticism, Rawal also condemned the abusive messages sent to Chopra following his remarks, highlighting the importance of respectful discourse.

title: Argentina risk FIFA punishment after Falkland Islands banner during England win celebrations
content: Argentina may face FIFA disciplinary action after displaying a Falklands sovereignty banner.  The team celebrated a World Cup semi-final victory over England with the controversial message.  This political display follows a similar incident for which Argentina was previously fined.  Argentina's vice-president supported the players' actions on social media platforms.  The team now prepares for the World Cup final against Spain.

title: 'To keep troops at absolute best': US announces testosterone screening for service members
content: US defense secretary Pete Hegseth announced a new annual testosterone screening program. This initiative will target military personnel aged thirty and older for better performance. Service members under thirty can volunteer for testing, while therapy remains voluntary. Medical experts note testosterone decline with age, impacting various health aspects, while critics question the policy.

title: 'Very cute': US President Donald Trump welcomes new $1 coin with his face on it
content: A new US dollar coin will feature President Donald Trump for America's 250th birthday. This commemorative coin is scheduled for release in the autumn of 2026. The design includes Trump's image and traditional American symbols on both sides. Critics raised concerns about this move conflicting with an 1866 law. The Treasury Department stated the coin is legal under a 2020 act.

title: FIFA WC: Messi shines as Argentina pull off stunner against England to reach final
content: Argentina clinched a dramatic 2-1 win against England on Wednesday, ensuring their place in the FIFA World Cup Final. The defending champions staged a remarkable comeback after trailing for a significant part of the match, as England's lead crumbled in the final moments. Next up for Argentina is a face-off against Spain in the grand finale this Sunday.

title: Delhi high court judge, later Manipur CJ, had LPG agency during tenure
content: Justice Siddharth Mridul allegedly operated an LPG distributorship during his judicial service. This action appears to violate judicial ethics and conduct codes. Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited issued notices regarding the alleged contract violations. The agency dealership was eventually suspended by the oil company. The controversy involves a former judge and an oil marketing PSU.

title: Doctor killed in flat, mentally ill son found injured; family alleges wife's role
content: A doctor was fatally stabbed at his Dharwad residence on Wednesday night. His eight-year-old son, who is mentally challenged, was found severely injured nearby. Police have detained the doctor's wife, who is currently in shock. Relatives are alleging the wife's involvement in the tragic incident. Investigations are ongoing to determine the exact cause and timeline.

title: Oil prices extend gain for fourth day, stay beyond $85 amid Middle East chaos
content: Crude oil prices climbed for a fourth day, exceeding eighty-five dollars per barrel. Investors reacted to escalating United States and Iran conflict and shipping route security. United States strikes on Iran's missile sites and coastal defense positions occurred. This intensified worries over oil moving through the Strait of Hormuz. Analysts suggest prices could rise further depending on conflict developments.

title: Antonela Roccuzzo shares emotional family update as Messi stays focused on World Cup
content: Antonela Roccuzzo backed Lionel Messi and Argentina with heartfelt Instagram messages after their World Cup victories, while the Messi family also shared an update confirming Jorge Messi is recovering from health issues. Messi admitted he endured "difficult days" during the tournament, making Argentina's run to the World Cup semifinal even more emotional for the family.

title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's latest move reportedly leaves Blake Lively on the outside
content: Taylor Swift's reported fallout with Blake Lively has resurfaced after insiders claimed Blake felt isolated following Taylor and Travis Kelce's latest milestone. Sources say the legal battle involving Justin Baldoni strained their friendship, while Blake reportedly struggled seeing Taylor celebrate with other close friends. Neither Swift, Lively, nor Ryan Reynolds has publicly commented on the latest claims.

title: Bellingham slaps Argentina player on the head after England's semifinal defeat
content: England's Jude Bellingham clashed with Argentina players after their World Cup semifinal defeat.  A video showed Bellingham tapping defender Valentin Barco on the head.  The match saw England lose 2-1, ending their World Cup final hopes.  Argentina will now face Spain in the final for a second title.  England plays France in the third-place playoff match.

title: Dak Prescott, Drake Maye react as former Cowboys QB announces retirement
content: Will Grier has announced his retirement from professional football after seven seasons.  He played for six different franchises during his NFL career.  Grier shared his decision publicly through an Instagram post.  Teammates like Dak Prescott and Christian McCaffrey offered their congratulations.  His departure leaves a respected legacy among former colleagues.

title: 'We're gutted': Harry Kane reveals Tuchel's final instruction England failed to follow
content: Harry Kane has revealed Thomas Tuchel's final touchline instruction before England's World Cup semi-final collapse against Argentina. Despite taking the lead, Tuchel urged his players to keep attacking rather than defend. England instead conceded twice late on, with Kane admitting the team ultimately tried to hold on as another major tournament ended in heartbreaking fashion.

title: India-UK trade deal: British cars get cheaper but Scotch has to wait
content: British car prices are decreasing as the India-UK CETA agreement begins implementation. Imported Scotch and Indian whiskies will see price reductions after state excise clearances. Car manufacturers like JLR and McLaren have already announced significant price cuts on their vehicles. Alcohol taxes remain high, impacting the extent of price reductions for consumers. Companies will decide if duty benefits are passed on to customers.

title: Watch: US disables oil tanker heading to Iran as naval blockade intensifies
content: United States forces disabled an oil tanker attempting to reach Iran's Kharg Island port. This action occurred during the initial enforcement period of a reinstated naval blockade. The vessel ignored multiple warnings and attempted to violate the US blockade. US forces also launched strikes against Iranian military capabilities in the region. Iran has condemned the blockade and warned of wider energy disruption.

title: Jagannath Rath Yatra 2026: Top wishes, quotes, images, and WhatsApp status images
content: The Rath Yatra festival celebrates the chariot of Lord Jagannath, signifying devotion and unity. This occasion offers numerous wishes and messages for loved ones to share, where people worldwide perform rituals for Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra, and Goddess Subhadra.

title: Sule hints at support for govt’s delimitation bill as buzz about NCP (SP) moves grows
content: NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule indicated potential support for the delimitation legislation. She stated a final decision would follow discussions within the INDI bloc. Sule denied any political discussions or shifts towards the NDA government. Jayant Patil met the Chief Minister to discuss development and local issues. These meetings have fueled speculation about the NCP (SP)'s political alignment.

title: Kudankulam nuclear plant data breached, NPCIL says core systems untouched
content: Nuclear Power Corporation of India stated its core systems remain secure after a cyber incident. A ransomware group posted files related to the Kudankulam nuclear plant online. Reliance Group confirmed a partial data breach on a third-party provider's server. These leaked files are not connected to nuclear safety or security systems. The incident involved conventional balance of plant common service facilities.

title: BJP turns PM Modi visits to states into 'swachh' drives
content: Organized cleanliness drives now precede Prime Minister Modi's state visits across India. These initiatives transform pre-VVIP spruce-ups into public campaigns involving citizens and officials. Haryana launched a statewide drive ahead of Prime Minister Modi's upcoming visit to Jind. Similar campaigns were observed in Odisha and West Bengal during recent visits. The true success of these drives will be measured by sustained cleanliness after the visits conclude.

title: 2022 in mind, Congress may continue with Warring as Punjab party chief
content: The Congress party is not favoring a leadership change in its Punjab unit. This stance comes despite some functionaries demanding Amarinder Raja Warring's removal. Party leaders are wary of reversing recent appointments, which caused internal backlash. Dissidence presents a difficult situation for the Congress party ahead of polls. The party may try to persuade dissidents with assurances and future promises.

title: Nearly 10,000 more MBBS seats up for grabs this year
content: India's MBBS capacity reaches a record 1,36,939 seats for the 2026-27 academic session. This expansion is driven by new regulations allowing earlier seat increase applications. The National Medical Commission approved 9,911 additional undergraduate seats across the nation. These new seats include those in newly established and existing medical colleges. Private institutions contributed significantly to this substantial increase in medical seats.

title: "Human Rights Disaster": Detainees At ICE's Texas Facility Allege Abuse
content: 90% of detainees interviewed at ICE's Fort Bliss facility said they had either personally been assaulted by the staff or had seen others physically abused, as per a report.

title: PM Modi's Rs 25,000 Crore Bet: Ganga, Varuna Corridors To Transform Kashi
content: The projects come at a time when Varanasi is witnessing a surge in visitors. The city attracts nearly 15 crore tourists and pilgrims every year.

title: Delhi Cop Installing Speed Monitoring Device Gets Killed By Speeding Car
content: The incident took place at around 6 pm at the Singhu border in outer north Delhi when the policeman was installing a speed monitoring machine near a traffic booth.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex, Nifty Open Flat As US Launches Fresh Strikes
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices rise for 4th session amid escalating US-Iran war; Brent crude at $85.28.

title: Why Europe's Deadly Heatwave Is More Than Just A Summer Crisis
content: A persistent high-pressure system, combined with warmer-than-average sea surface temperatures, has trapped an immense mass of hot air over Europe, driving extreme temperatures across the continent.

title: US Ropes In Ex-RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan, 2 Other Indians For Key Fed Roles
content: Rajan will serve on the balance sheet policy task force to examine the costs, benefits, and institutional implications of the Federal Reserve's current balance sheet regime, including its asset holdings and their role in implementing monetary policy.

title: Pune Cops Visit Rajasthan Temple to Verify Siya-Chetan Secret Marriage Claim
content: Pune Police visited Khatu Shyam Temple to verify reports of a secret marriage between Ketan Agarwal murder accused Siya Goyal and Chetan Chowdhury

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train Bridge Unaffected By Rains, Clarify Officials
content: Rejecting media reports claiming that a temporary bridge erected for the construction of the main bridge was washed away, officials clarified that the affected structure was only a temporary access arrangement

title: Amid Ram Temple Theft Row, Mansa Devi Priests Get Pocketless Clothes
content: Mansa Devi Temple Trust Chairman Ravindra Puri welcomed the new dress code, stating that all priests and staff members have since adhered to the requirement of wearing pocketless attire.

title: "Can't Give Up": Trump Denies Removing Immigration Traffic Stops
content: It's not clear whether ICE will quickly reverse course and resume most stops, which have been a key tool in Trump's immigration crackdown.

title: Don't Sign A Rent Agreement Until You Review These 3 Hidden Clauses First
content: Legal experts say that overlooking just a few clauses can later lead to disputes over security deposits, maintenance charges or even hefty penalties.

title: Siya Goyal's Family's Shop Sealed Over No License, Adulteration Charge
content: A stock of 4,172 kg of these products, valued at Rs 8.14 lakh, was seized during the inspection due to suspected contravention in labelling and possible adulteration, the FDA said.

title: Iran Claims It Downed MQ-9 Drone As US Strikes Damage Children's Cancer Hospital
content: IRGC has claimed it has intercepted and destroyed an "enemy" MQ-9 drone over the southwestern city of Andimeshk

title: 115 Fall Ill After Eating Street Golgappas, 12 Hospitalised In Rajasthan
content: Around 115 people, including a large number of children, fell ill after consuming golgappas (pani-patasi) from a street vendor in Polai Khurd village under the Simliya area of Digod subdivision in Kota district.

title: Supreme Court Clears UP's Rs 1,500-Crore Kukrail Night Safari Project
content: Divided into two phases, the ambitious project is India's first urban night safari at the sprawling 5,000-hectare Kukrail Reserve Forest and will cost about Rs 1,500 crore.

title: Venezuela Earthquake: Death Count Rises To 4,829, Over 20,000 Displaced
content: The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes struck within a minute of each other on June 24, decimating the coastal state of La Guaira located north of Caracas.

title: Amid War, Trump Says Iran Has Freed Detained US Citizen In Goodwill Gesture
content: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that arch foe Iran has freed an American citizen detained since December 2024 and allowed her to leave the country.

title: Blasts Heard In Kyiv As EU Chief Visits Ukraine Amid Russian Missile Alert
content: A series of explosions rang out in Kyiv shortly after midnight on Thursday, an AFP journalist said, after a Ukrainian air force alert reported the approach of several ballistic missiles.

title: Rs 5 Lakh Paid To NTA Paper-Setter In NEET Leak, 111 Questions Matched: CBI
content: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has recovered Motegaonkar's phone in which 36 images (five duplicate images) containing 132 handwritten questions of chemistry were found, the agency said.

title: 3 Policemen Killed, 34 Injured In 2 Terrorist Attacks In Pakistan
content: Terrorists attacked a police convoy in Lower Dir district with hand grenades and automatic weapons on Wednesday, killing three police personnel and injuring 19.

title: 'There were mistakes': Acting US attorney general Blanche grilled over Trump ties and Epstein files
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Proposed apartment law may not be perfect, but it is a good start: Greater Bengaluru Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda
content: Mr. Gowda pointed out that the existing laws were enacted over 50 years ago, and the city had grown by leaps and bounds since then.

title: Women's quota, Delimitation Bills will be passed in Monsoon Session: Athawale
content: We have a two-thirds majority. Women's Reservation Bill and Delimitation Bill will be passed, says Union Minister Ramdas Athawale

title: Apartment owners in Bengaluru get briefing on new law for property owners from Karnataka CM
content: He informed that the proposed Karnataka Apartment (Ownership and Management) Act, 2026 has been drafted to protect the interests of homebuyers, and ensure complete ownership rights

title: Latur coaching owner paid ₹5 lakh to procure NEET questions from NTA paper-setter, says CBI
content: The CBI has found that 111 of these questions allegedly match with the questions in the NTA’s master question sets prepared for NEET (UG) 2026

title: India-U.K. DCC not applicable to Indians working in the U.K. before July 15
content: DCC came into effect on July 15, 2026 alongside the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA)

title: Gujarat announces rehabilitation package for Surat flood-affected traders
content: The package provides lump-sum financial assistance to street vendors, handcart operators and owners of small and large cabins to help resume business operations in the affected areas

title: Madras High Court nullifies registration of land belonging to Palani math
content: A Division Bench of Justices C.V. Karthikeyan and R. Sakthivel allowed the appeal preferred by the mutt against a Single Bench order that had directed the Sub-Registrar to register the sale deed if the document was otherwise in order

title: Sonam Wangchuk’s decision to join CJP protest organic, says wife Gitanjali Angmo
content: Sonam Wangchuk wanted to ensure that the CJP’s cause is not sabotaged by “nefarious” elements, and the movement does not go astray, says his wife Gitanjali J. Angmo

title: On the Grand Road of Puri
content: The Jagannatha temple in the city in Odisha is an impressive place of worship, and the grand festival of the deity is incomparable

title: NPCIL denies ‘sensitive data breach’ at Kudankulam nuclear plant project
content: Sources in the KKNPP say the alleged data breach has triggered ‘absolute commotion’ among the project’s top brass

title: Why is Telangana still waiting for monsoon rain despite showers in neighbouring States?
content: International climate monitoring agencies continue to indicate that El Nino conditions are expected to strengthen during the remainder of the southwest monsoon season and later in the year, says IMD Hyderabad Senior Meteorologist Srinivas G.N.R.S

title: Indian national stabbed in U.S. over his religion: reports
content: Syed Sohailuddin, a worker in the Valley City Mall in West Valley City, Utah, was allegedly stabbed by Peter Michael Larsen, who asked the victim his religion, Fox 13 News reported

title: Police summon ex-officials in probe into missing inquiry reports from Odisha CMO
content: The two former officers have been asked to appear before the investigating officer of the Capital Police Station in Bhubaneswar at 11 a.m. on July 22

title: Bihar cabinet authorises Gram Panchayats to levy and collect taxes in Panchayats
content: Nod given to draft “Gram Panchayat Taxes, Rates and Fees Rules, 2026” for the purpose; cabinet also gave its nod for the delimitation of Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti and Zila Parishad

title: Gyanvapi dispute resolution set for the long haul, with both sides saying no to mediation
content: Next steps would now entail a renewed process of formal court hearings, procedural requirements, frequent adjournments, and delays in evidence collection

title: Nagpur Cyber Police register FIR against content creators over alleged defamatory campaign targeting Nitin Gadkari
content: The legal action stems from highly contested online posts regarding the government's Ethanol-blended (E20) fuel policy

title: Beyond momos: Meghalaya’s chefs are reclaiming the state’s culinary identity
content: In Shillong, chefs behind A’Origins, Rynsan and Lady Aiko are reclaiming Meghalaya’s culinary heritage with dishes built around fermented bamboo shoots, smoked meats, forest produce and traditional cooking techniques.

title: Low water level in Mullaperiyar raising concern in both States
content: The low storage impacts not only Kerala but also neighbouring districts in Tamil Nadu; it has hit farming activities and may soon pose a threat to tourism and drinking water distribution in the Kumily and Chakkupallom panchayats

title: NDA steps up push for numbers ahead of delimitation Bill, Opposition closes ranks
content: Both the ruling alliance and Opposition INDIA bloc are firming up strategies ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament; NDA banks on splits in Trinamool and Sena and the realignment in T.N.; Congress’s parliamentary group is scheduled to meet today

title: ED files PMLA complaint in alleged visa fraud racket
content: The ED’s probe is based on multiple FIRs alleging conspiracy in the preparation of forged educational certificates, fabricated experience certificates, fake financial statements, and fraudulent proof of funds for securing U.S. student and visitor visas

title: Four Ayush colleges will be set up in A.P., says Health Minister
content: Integrated AYUSH hospitals will be set up at Tirupati, Giddalur, Nandigama, Tenali, Tekkali and Rampachodavaram, with an investment of ₹90 crore, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: As Sonam Wangchuk hangs on to hunger strike, weight loss approaches 9 kg
content: On the 18th day, activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his hunger strike demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, even as his vital signs showed a worsening condition

title: Intense heat pushes power consumption to 280 MUs per day in Andhra Pradesh
content: Steps have been taken to ensure an uninterrupted power supply, and disruptions are being promptly attended to by the staff of the power utilities, says Energy Minister

title: Order CBI probe in TVK MLA bribery case and other horse trading complaints, insists a PIL plea before Madras High Court
content: The case filed by a Tiruppur-based lawyer has been listed for hearing before Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan on Thursday

title: Andhra Pradesh student dies in road accident in U.S.A.
content: Prasanna was travelling in a car which lost control and crashed into a tree, resulting in her death on the spot

title: Another COVID-19 death reported in A.P., three more test positive
content: The death of 66-year-old man has been attributed to comorbidities, and not the COVID-19 infection alone

title: S. Janaki’s voice will echo forever, says Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: The government will introduce a comprehensive policy for the film industry soon, including single-window clearances for film shoots and support for infrastructure development, says Kandula Durgesh

title: Family of missing seaman want Centre to take steps to locate him
content: His family could not get in touch with him from March 26 this year when he was aboard the merchant vessel M.V. PERSISTENSE

title: TNCC hopes for a revival in Tamil Nadu, with new president and new alliance
content: The party’s headquarters is now uncharacteristically buzzing with activity as cadre and visitors are vying to meet the new president and two new ministers

title: Worker rolls shrink by 67.6 lakh in first fortnight after MGNREGS transition, finds LibTech
content: Analysis finds decline in registered and active workers coinciding with shift to VB-G RAM G; Rural Development Ministry counters claim saying it has received no complaints

title: Farmers stage motorcycle rally to protest against proposed India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers behind the ‘Desh Bachao Morcha’ urge the Central government to ‘prioritise the interests of farmers, workers, small traders, small industries and India’s economic and food sovereignty, and refrain from implementing any agreement that goes against the interests of the people’

title: Kalaignar Centenary government boarding, lodging facility in Vellore remains closed due to delay in fixing room rents
content: At present, tourists and patients from surrounding districts and States pay exorbitant rates for poorly maintained rental rooms in cramped private mansions and motels

title: Low demand for B.Sc. Mathematics and B.A. Economics led to the closure of these courses, says Ambur college principal
content: At present, the college, which is affiliated to Thiruvalluvar University in Vellore, offers at least five aided UG courses

title: Two get life imprisonment in Guntur dowry death case
content: Special court for the trial of crimes against women convicts two persons in a dowry harassment case that led to the death of a woman and her daughter in 2020

title: Robbery at ATM in Ranipet, ₹7 lakh stolen
content: When bank officials noticed that there were no withdrawals at the ATM, they reached the outlet and found its shutter closed from the outside

title: Man ends life in Palnadu; police deny allegations of harassment
content: Amid allegations that a note purportedly written by the deceased blamed police harassment, police say that wife of the victim was summoned to police station in connection with a land dispute case

title: Bengaluru: Police nab four inter-State drug peddlers who challenged cops; MDMA worth ₹34 crore seized
content: One of the accused, a Nigerian national, had allegedly posted on social media that he would continue peddling drugs in the State despite the police crackdown.

title: VIT gets ₹50 lakh endowment fund for student innovation programmes
content: The initiative will extend beyond financial assistance by offering industry mentorship, startup guidance, and leadership development opportunities

title: SC declines to interfere with HC relief to ex-DMK Minister E.V. Velu in Highways scam case
content: The DVAC registered the FIR against Mr. Velu, certain government officials and a contractor on June 24 based on a complaint filed in 2022 by anti-corruption NGO Arappor Iyakkam
